video_title: Unlocking the Secrets: The Air Suspension System of Land Rover Discovery 3 Explained
transcript: [Music] hello and welcome back to the Overland Legend I'm Alan this is my Discovery three that I've had for about well I've had it for almost 11 years now but uh 2 years ago I did a video on what it was like owning this vehicle and maintaining it that was sort of the the 9-year period that I'd had and it's been 2 years since then and I've had some more maintenance uh and and some more uh repair issues and then I've also got some unresolved issues that I'm I'm trying to sort out um so yeah the the main problems I've had again were were suspension related but the the good news is that I finally figured out how this system works and I want to share that with you but before I do that let me just talk about some of the maintenance that I did and then I've had to do some repairs to the coolant system and then I'll get to at the end just share those sort of problems that I've got that are they're not major problems but they're things that I just don't know how to solve and uh and maybe you can give me some advice on that so let's have a look and see what I've done in the last two years on this vehicle a the mileage is currently sitting on 317,000 I have maintained the normal service schedule as discussed the last time except that I've started using the 5w 40 oil instead of 5W30 the logic being that with an older engine the slightly thicker oil will maintain better oil pressure I can't say that I've noticed any difference though I did replace the tires again at 270,000 as there was some sidewall damage and they developed flat spots I had the General Grabber ATS on but decided to change to the goodier Wrangler all terrains even though I had been rotating the tires every 12,000 km I had not been doing wheel alignment or balancing so since replacing the tires I have been rotating and balancing and aligning every 12,000 km and now I've done about 45,000 K on this new set and they still looking quite good on a trip to Mosen beak I had a nervous moment when the coolant level indicator came on we were in the middle of nowhere and it was getting hot so I stopped and checked it out but the coolant was full and everything seemed normal so we just kept on going okay so I had that low coolant level indicator on my uh Dash and um I got it when I was on a trip in Mosen Beek and it seems to have come back again um and the cause is typically the little float that's in the bottom of the um tank and you can see that float there and it moves up and down like that and what happens is it gets stuck uh because it starts swelling up and then uh and then it shows low coolant level but actually it's there the coolant is fine so you can see I don't have any issues with the coolant but it's showing low coolant level so um you need to yeah so I have to replace the whole bottle because it comes with the sensor so this is an aftermarket one that I got it's supposed to be one of the better quality ones um so that's what I'll replace so now just uh replacing it and what I've decided to do is take take the headlight out just to give me a bit more space um so it's quite an easy operation to do that you just have to undo these um four Clips at the top and then there's another clip on the actual um housing that you just push it up and it Clips on you so you can pull that off quite easily and then to take the headlight out um you just have to um there's a on the headlight itself there's these uh these you just pull them up and the and they slot in over there so it slots into those slots over there and and over here and then it just uh pulls off it just pulls off from there and then uh you just disconnect the the cable and then it's loose and then to take the bottle out hopefully it's going to be fairly easy I've watched a few YouTube videos and some of them were quite good so I just need to undo these bolts here um there little locating pins there which need to come out and then what I'm going to do is I'm going to clamp off these hoses is this hose and uh the bottom hose down here I'm going to clamp that hose off also as well so I'm not changing the coolant I'll just lose the coolant in the bottle and then I'll refill so that's what my plan is so let's see how that works [Music] [Applause] [Music] out okay that was a lot easier than I thought uh it actually went very nicely but uh thanks to yeah I'll drop the links for some of those videos that I saw they they're really helpful uh especially the uh British Atlantic uh spares one which is where I worked out to use a a clamp to clamp the the pipes off so I clamped those pipes off and there was no leakage uh I just when I took it off I did forget to just disconnect the um the sensor wire at the bottom so but that was easy enough after that and then I disconnected it and and and took it out and was really easy and but there's definitely one tool that you have to have um you have to have this uh it's a special crimping tool that's uh used for for these clamps it's like a remote tool so it's it works remotely and it's easy to get down into that hole down there um and and then also it it actually locks in place so so it holds it locked and you can uh take it really easily and then put it back on so yeah without this this thing uh would have been a nightmare and so that was well worth it to get that but easy enough so hopefully now coolant level uh problem is solved about a year later I had the same problem again so I got the coolant loss warning on my dashboard and but this time the the coolant had actually dropped below the level so the gauge was working working properly and anyway so I filled it up I think it was about 600 Ms of coolant that I put back in again and then drove it for a while and and then again it it dropped off and I had a look everywhere and I couldn't see a leak there was nothing obvious on the ground I couldn't see any wet pipes or anything so there was clearly a slow leak somewhere so I actually took it in to the Land Rover guys to have a look where they could pressure test it properly and the result was the pressure test showed that the radi rator was actually leaking and so I've had to replace the radiator so that that wasn't a cheap exercise but I've uh it's just one of those things I guess the the radiator while was more than 11 years old now so but that was the leak it was a very small leak anyway we fixed it replaced the radiator and since then Sol uh problem has been solved one thing I didn't mention in my last video is um I actually had to change the oil uh temperature sensor it sits on the sump actually right next to the drain plug just on the side you can't see it now cuz I've still got the cover on here and I haven't taken it off but the symptom for that is is rather odd because it's like your vehicle when you when I started it the revs would go up and down almost uncontrollably almost like it was doing it itself and that's a definite um symptom of the oil temperature sensor so it's it's a fairly easy thing to replace you literally just unscrew it and and put a new one in so that is something that I did change and uh and that solved the problem immediately okay now let's get on to some more of those suspension problems that I've been having so this is the suspension drop on the front that I seem to be experiencing randomly this is after yesterday I probably got home about 4:00 and now today it's about 10:00 and now it looks like this but before I've also left it for for two days then it doesn't go down so and it's mostly more down on the left hand side there were no suspension faults coming up so all the components were working properly so it had to be a leak somewhere this can actually be quite difficult to solve because this vehicle's got a self-leveling feature so so periodically it checks to see what the pressure is on the different wheels and then levels everything to to level it out so if you've got a leak on the one side you you're not going to detect it because the whole vehicle will settle a little bit the way to uh check that is if you disable the leveling feature and you can do that by just removing fuse um the fuse is number 26 in the fuse box so 26 if you remove that you can see the one with a little arrow on the suspension 20 amp which is this one over here and if we pull that one out then it the leveling feature won't work anymore so to test it you just pull that out so when I did put that take that fuse out and I had a look it wasn't quite conclusive because it was dropping uh a little bit on both sides but probably a little bit more on the left hand side but it wasn't really conclusive but to make matters worse I had some previous history with this suspension so the very first major suspension problem that I had was the the whole vehicle dropped and and had the warning signs and everything and I immediately suspected the airbags and so I went ahead and changed the airbags and it turned out not to be the airbags it was actually the valve Block in the front and then the two height sensors on the on the front on the left and the right hand side and so that was a really expensive mistake that I made uh simply by not understanding the system so this time I was convinced that it wouldn't be the airbags but rather the valve block seeing as I just recently replaced the airbags or so I thought I ordered a valve block oing kit from Air seals UK the front valve block sits behind the front wheel Arch I took the whole wheel Arch off to get it out which makes it easier to work on but you can actually just bend back the bottom bit to get access as well make sure that you support the vehicle chassis on axle stands before you let the air out because that's all that's holding the vehicle up okay so now I can get to this front valve and that's the one that might be the problem so I've got the kit for that now so now I must just take off these pipes and then open it see it's all the original Land Rover one I know they changed it I had a problem before and they actually changed it so they did use the original unless they never changed it but you never know so anyway now I'm just going to change the seals that just pops in there so it's quite easy to get the cover off and then to get to that is easy enough there are many useful YouTube videos explaining this in detail and you can see the links in the description below with the valve block off I took it apart and cleaned it out and replaced all of the O-rings the instructions that came with the o-ring kit from Air seals UK was excellent and they also have some videos on their website that you can get access to once you've bought the kit [Music] [Music] [Music] I refitted the valve block reconnected the pipes and recharged the air system I had to leave it overnight again to see what happens but to my disappointment it was still lowering at first I suspected that I hadn't done the seal replacement in the valve block properly so I got some soapy water and did a leak test but found nothing then I also did a leak test on the airbags and that's when I saw the bubbles on the front left airbag so that was the culprit when I checked it was 5 years ago that I'd actually replaced these airbags so it had been quite a while I got two new front air bags and replaced them myself having done this before I knew that it was a fairly simple process the hardest part is loosening and connecting the air pipes on top of the airspring it's a little bit fiddly to get your hands and and a spanner in there to loosen it and then to reconnect it and tighten it but with a little bit of care and patience it's quite doable this solved the problem and everything is now working as it should after all those suspension problems that I've had I've actually finally understood how this system works now and once you understand it it's it's quite simple and you know what the different parts and the different components do and what errors and what faults they could have and what could lead to what problems so let me explain to you exactly how this system works and hopefully it will help you the main components are the air compressor the reservoir tank and then the four airbags that are connected via air supply hose to control the air flow leveling lowering and raising you've got three valve blocks there's the valve block the reservoir valve block which sits inside the compressor compartment then you've got the front valve block which sits behind the front uh wheel Arch and then you've got the rear valve block which sits at the rear of the vehicle to control the height you've got the four height sensors which sit at Each corner uh at each of the wheels and then all of this is controlled electric electrically by the air suspension control module which is operated via the switch that sits between the seats the reservoir valve block manages the Air Supply from the compressor to and from the reservoir and then the Air Supply from the front and the rear valve blocks the connections from the compress to the front and rear control valves are connected via a common Gallery within the valve and therefore they all actually operate at the same pressure the front and rear valve blocks control and adjust the pressure of each of the airsprings and then they control the leveling of the height between the left and the right air Springs via a crossover valve so if you have de warnings for suspension then it's most likely an electrical fault which is related to the valves or the height sensors or the pump itself and to diagnose those you'd need to look at the fault codes to identify which part of the electrical system of the suspension is at fault these faults can give you limp mode as well because the system then tries to protect itself the other type of fault that you can have is when there's no actual warnings on the dashboard so then you've got a slow leak or you've got height changes between wheels and then what you can do is disconnect the electrical system and see if that problem persists and then more than likely you've got a leak in the system so that's a it's probably a little bit harder to find but it's a it's it's a simpler arguably a simpler fix but it could be a leak in the actual airbag it could be a leak in one of the pipes in one of the connectors it could be a leak in the valve from being getting dirty and and the seals not working properly then of course you can have both of those together so that's the worst case scenario where you've got electrical faults and you've got a leak and then that's obviously going to be quite difficult to to solve but if you follow that simple process of trying to isolate each problem and identify it you'll be able to solve it okay now onto those unresolved problems that I've got so maybe you can help me out with trying to identify those and and if you have experienced it or if you have any ideas then please leave the comment in the leave a comment in the comments below and maybe we can try and find a solution to some of these problems the first is my crack dashboard this is a 2009 model So it's 15 years old now and I've owned it for 11 years and always stored it undercover in the day and night but despite that the dash has cracked around the passenger airbag this is a fairly common problem now you can patch it with strips and glue but that would look bad and I could remove it and have it recovered but I can't find people who have done this before I could also replace it with a secondhand unit but how long will that last you can buy molded covers to fit on top but how well they fit and look I'm not sure also what happens to the airbag when it deploys and then of course you could spend a fortune to replace it with a new one so what would you do or recommend or what have you done the next one is some knocking and play in the steering I only feel it on rough gravel roads mostly at low speed I've checked the lower steering components and there's no play there and I even replaced the lower steering column again but that made no change I know it's not coming from the swing arm bushes because I know what that feels like and that's more of a knock on the vehicle and not felt in the steering so I assume that it's the upper steering column is that even possible and is it a known problem and would changing this solve the problem and how easy is this job to do maybe you can help me out by leaving some comments or your experience then the last unresolved problem is a starting problem when I restart soon after driving the vehicle starts immediately but it's a bit rough as if it's being starved of fuel and takes a few seconds to get up to smooth idle it mainly seems to happen on warmer days and only after I've been driving for a while and then stop but restart again after 5 or 10 minutes I even went so far as to check the fuel pressure from the pump in the tank and that seems to be working correctly with the right power supply and the right pressure I don't have the the same problem if the vehicle has been standing for a while or overnight there's also no other problem driving or accelerating it's a real mystery and happens most of the time but not every time well that's it as far as my disco 3 goes if you've got any advice on those unresolved problems then please let me know in the comments below and until next time see you later bye oh [Music]
